title: executor-capabilities-v1
description: The executor's self-description, executor -> platform, consumed by the compatibility handshake before any launch (ADR-001, verified update of 2026-07-16). It answers two questions the platform must not guess: which contract document versions this binary speaks, and which SSH capabilities it has — as distinct facts, never one boolean. Unlike execution-event-v1 and execution-result-v1 this document is STRICT at every level: its field names legitimately contain sensitive substrings ('password', 'private_key'), so the recursive redaction walk cannot apply; a closed, fully-typed vocabulary is what guarantees no extra field can carry a secret. Evolving it means a new format_version, never a silently-added key. Semantic validators: internal/executioncontract/capabilities.go and domain/execution_contract.py.

format_version.description: Document format version. Absent, zero, and future versions are rejected; there is no silent downgrade.

executor_version.description: The binary's build version (internal/version.String()). Never the document format version. Must not be blank.

contract.description: Per document kind, every format_version this binary can produce or consume. The platform launches only when the version it needs appears in every list.

ssh.description: Distinct authentication and trust facts. A capability is a statement about the engine's code (config.go XOR validation, sshx/auth.go, KnownFields-strict host.yaml, known_hosts derived from HOME), never a value set by hand to unblock a launch.
